title: Paths, Navigation, and Filesystem Hierarchy · Spiral Review
summary: Locate application configuration and logs on a workstation whose current directory and distribution conventions differ from a familiar machine. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a path-resolution and filesystem-map notebook after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Resolve absolute, relative, home-relative, dot, and parent paths from a stated working directory.
Use pwd, cd, and directory stacks while predicting the resulting working directory before each transition.
Relate root-level directories to boot, configuration, devices, variable data, user data, runtime state, and optional software roles.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence
steps.instruction:
review case — On a review host, learner starts in /home/learner/lab with Bash 5.3, Coreutils 9.11, and exit status 1. Resolve ./data/../scripts, identify command authority, and separate terminal, shell, process, and kernel evidence. Competency probe: Choose paths from documented interfaces and discovered configuration rather than hard-coded workstation assumptions.

Inspect the Path Portability trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for choose paths from documented interfaces and discovered configuration rather than hard-coded workstation assumptions.
